title: Pork and apple pie
subtitle: A double-crust pie of seasoned pork mince and apple
excerpt: Pork and apple pie is a deep double-crust shortcrust pie filled with well-seasoned pork mince, sharp apple, onion, and sage. The filling is part-cooked before it goes in, so the bake is purely about the pastry. The apple provides acidity that cuts through the fatty pork; the sage is essential.
sourceNotes: Pork and apple is a classic British flavour combination with a long history in British domestic cookery. Double-crust pork pies appear in British recipes from the seventeenth century onward. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932), Project Gutenberg, documents regional pork pie traditions. The form here follows twentieth-century British domestic cooking convention.

recipe.freezeNotes: Bake and cool completely. Freeze in a sealed container. Reheat from frozen in a 170°C oven for 25 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the filling up to 2 days ahead. Assemble and bake on the day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; drop to 180°C after 20 minutes

content.text: Pork and apple pie is a double-crust pie: a base and lid of shortcrust pastry enclosing a filling of pork mince, chopped apple, and sage. The filling is part-cooked on the hob first, so the bake time is purely about the pastry. A sharp apple such as Bramley or Cox gives the acidity that the rich pork needs; a sweet dessert apple will not do.

Rub the cold butter into the flour and salt until the mixture looks like coarse sand. Add the cold water and bring together into a firm dough. Divide into two portions, two-thirds for the base and one-third for the lid. Wrap both and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Soften the onion in a little oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the pork mince and cook, breaking it up, until no longer pink. Add the apple and sage, season generously, and cook for 5 minutes until the apple has softened slightly. The filling should not be saucy; drain any excess liquid. Cool completely before assembling.

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Roll the large pastry portion and line a deep 23 cm pie dish. Fill with the cold pork mixture, mounding it slightly in the centre. Roll the lid, lay it on top, press the edges together, and trim. Crimp the edge and cut steam vents. Brush with beaten egg. Bake for 20 minutes, then drop the heat to 180°C and bake for a further 30 minutes until the pastry is deep gold and crisp. Rest for 15 minutes before cutting.
